name: NASA CEA (Chemical Equilibrium with Applications)
description: NASA's free thermochemical equilibrium code for calculating rocket engine performance. Computes combustion temperature, exhaust composition, and specific impulse for any propellant combination.
whyUseIt: CEA is the starting point for every rocket engine design. Before you design a single hardware component, you need to know what your propellant combination produces in terms of temperature, pressure, and exhaust properties. CEA gives you these numbers in seconds.

vocabulary.definition: Oxidiser-to-Fuel mass ratio — the primary parameter that determines combustion performance
Assumption that exhaust composition is fixed after the throat — gives conservative Isp estimates

beginnerMistakes: Using shifting equilibrium when frozen flow is more appropriate for small engines
Not checking if the O/F ratio is in the practical range for the propellant
Ignoring combustion instability considerations that CEA cannot predict
